description_long: This object is a heavily weathered and rusted tool resembling a pipe wrench, with a cylindrical wooden handle and a metallic head featuring an adjustable jaw mechanism. The handle is thick, rounded, and shows signs of wear, with patches of paint or finish missing, exposing the underlying wood. The head is metallic, with a dark, corroded surface and visible adjustment components, including a knurled knob and a sliding jaw. The tool's overall appearance is utilitarian, with no visible branding or decorative elements, and the metal parts are pitted and stained from extensive use. The proportions suggest it is hand-held, with the handle designed for a firm grip and the head for gripping or turning pipes. There are no visible chains, cutting bars, or safety guards typical of a chainsaw, and the object lacks any clear power source indicators.
description: A rusted, worn hand tool with a wooden handle and a corroded metal head, resembling a pipe wrench. The tool shows heavy use and lacks any visible chainsaw features.
description_view_0: The tool is viewed head-on, showing the circular end of the handle and the front of the metallic jaw mechanism.
description_view_1: A side profile reveals the full length of the wooden handle and the detailed structure of the adjustable metal head.
description_view_2: The rear view highlights the rounded end of the handle and the back of the tool's head, with adjustment components visible.
description_view_3: The opposite side profile displays the handle's wear and the side of the metal head, including the adjustment knob and jaw.
attribute_handle_design: Single cylindrical wooden handle, rounded at the end, with no visible ergonomic or safety features.
